questions.text: Streaming of protoplasm in Unicellular organisms like Amoeba is a form of:
questions.explanation: Amoeboid movement is achieved by pseudopodia formed by the streaming of protoplasm.

questions.text: Which of the following cells in the human body exhibit amoeboid movement?
questions.explanation: Some specialized cells like macrophages in blood help in defense via amoeboid movement.

questions.text: Ciliary movement is seen in the human body in:
questions.explanation: Cilia help in removing dust particles from the trachea and in moving the ova through the fallopian tubes.

questions.text: What percentage of body weight of an adult human is contributed by muscles?
questions.explanation: Muscles are a major part of the human body mass, making up nearly half of the weight.

questions.text: Skeletal muscles are also called ________ muscles because of their appearance under the microscope.
questions.explanation: They show alternating light and dark bands (striations).

questions.text: The plasma membrane of a muscle fiber is called the:
questions.explanation: The sarcolemma encloses the sarcoplasm of the muscle fiber.

questions.text: The functional unit of contraction in a muscle fiber is the:
questions.explanation: A sarcomere is the portion of a myofibril between two successive Z-lines.

questions.text: The dark band of a sarcomere, containing both actin and myosin, is the:
questions.explanation: The A-band (Anisotropic) contains the entire length of the thick (myosin) filament.

questions.text: Thin filaments of a muscle fiber are composed of which protein?
questions.explanation: Thin filaments are made of Actin, along with regulatory proteins Troponin and Tropomyosin.

questions.text: The 'H-zone' in the center of the A-band consists of:
questions.explanation: The H-zone is the central part of the thick filament not overlapped by thin filaments.

questions.text: Each myosin filament is a polymerized protein made of monomeric units called:
questions.explanation: Meromyosins have two parts: Heavy Meromyosin (HMM) and Light Meromyosin (LMM).

questions.text: Which protein masks the active binding sites for myosin on the actin filaments in a resting state?
questions.explanation: Troponin is a complex protein that blocks the myosin binding sites on actin.

questions.text: The mechanism of muscle contraction is best explained by the:
questions.explanation: This theory states that contraction occurs by the sliding of thin filaments over the thick filaments.

questions.text: Which ion binds to troponin to initiate muscle contraction?
questions.explanation: Calcium binding to troponin changes its shape, uncovering the binding sites on actin.

questions.text: The energy required for muscle contraction comes from the hydrolysis of:
questions.explanation: The myosin head acts as an ATPase enzyme to hydrolyze ATP.

questions.text: Fatigue in muscles occurs due to the accumulation of:
questions.explanation: Repeated activation leads to anaerobic breakdown of glycogen, producing lactic acid.

questions.text: Red muscle fibers are rich in an oxygen-storing pigment called:
questions.explanation: Myoglobin gives the red appearance and allows these fibers to work aerobically for long periods.

questions.text: The total number of bones in an adult human skeletal system is:
questions.explanation: The skeletal system is divided into Axial (80 bones) and Appendicular (126 bones).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT part of the axial skeleton?
questions.explanation: Girdles and limbs form the appendicular skeleton.

questions.text: The only movable bone in the human skull is the:
questions.explanation: The mandible (lower jaw) is the only bone that moves, facilitating eating and speaking.

questions.text: The first vertebra is called the:
questions.explanation: The atlas articulates with the occipital condyles of the skull.

questions.text: How many pairs of ribs are called 'floating ribs'?
questions.explanation: The 11th and 12th pairs of ribs are not connected ventrally to the sternum.

questions.text: The bone of the upper arm is the:
questions.explanation: The humerus articulates with the pectoral girdle at the shoulder.

questions.text: The longest and strongest bone in the human body is the:
questions.explanation: The femur (thigh bone) supports the weight of the body.

questions.text: The joint between the atlas and axis is a:
questions.explanation: The pivot joint allows for the rotational movement of the head.

questions.text: Which disorder is characterized by inflammation of joints due to accumulation of uric acid crystals?
questions.explanation: Gout is a metabolic disorder affecting the joints.

questions.text: Osteoporosis is an age-related disease characterized by:
options: Decreased bone mass and increased fractures
questions.explanation: Decreased levels of estrogen is a common cause in elderly women.

questions.text: The 'Cup-shaped' cavity in the pelvic girdle that articulates with the femur is:
questions.explanation: The acetabulum is where the three bones of the pelvic girdle meet.

questions.text: Sutures in the skull are examples of ________ joints.
questions.explanation: Fibrous joints do not allow any movement.

questions.text: Autoimmune disorder affecting the neuromuscular junction leading to fatigue and paralysis of skeletal muscles is:
questions.explanation: Myasthenia gravis is a chronic autoimmune disease.

questions.text: The U-shaped bone at the base of the buccal cavity is:
questions.explanation: The hyoid bone does not articulate with any other bone.

questions.text: Number of cervical vertebrae in almost all mammals is:
questions.explanation: Even a giraffe has only 7 cervical vertebrae.

questions.text: The small globular head of a meromyosin contains binding sites for:
questions.explanation: The head of the thick filament interacts with actin to form cross-bridges.

questions.text: Which structure connects a muscle to a bone?
questions.explanation: Tendons are dense fibrous connective tissues that attach muscles to bones.

questions.text: The part of the myofibril between two Z-lines consists of:
options: One A-band and half of two I-bands
questions.explanation: This is the structural composition of a single sarcomere.

questions.text: Rapid spasms in muscle due to low Ca++ in body fluid is:
questions.explanation: Hypocalcemia leads to increased excitability of nerves and muscles, causing tetany.

questions.text: The glenoid cavity articulates with the head of the ________.
questions.explanation: This forms the shoulder joint (Ball and socket).

questions.text: Synovial joints are characterized by the presence of a:
questions.explanation: The synovial fluid reduces friction and allows free movement.

questions.text: Knee joint and Elbow joint are examples of:
questions.explanation: Hinge joints allow movement in only one plane.

questions.text: The total number of phalanges in one human hand is:
questions.explanation: Digit formula is 2, 3, 3, 3, 3. Total = 14.
